title: Executive Order 9540—Authorizing the Petroleum Administrator to Take the Possession of and to the Cities Service Refining Corporation, Located in and Around Lake Charles, Louisiana

flags.justification: The Petroleum Administrator may operate the plant 'in any manner... that he may deem necessary,' exercise any of the company's contractual rights, and (Sec. 6) delegate 'any and all power, authority, and discretion' to a Deputy — broad, largely unchecked operational authority.

Unlike comparable seizure orders in this corpus, Sec. 5 sets a concrete outer bound: possession terminates 'in no event more than sixty days after the restoration of productive efficiency' — a checkable, textually-anchored deadline exists, even though the underlying 'no longer required' determination is self-assessed.
Single coherent purpose: seizure of this one refinery to end a labor-disturbance-caused production interruption.
'In any manner, in accordance with applicable laws, that he may deem necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' (Sec. 1) is an open-ended operational standard with no defined limits.

No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable. Sub-flag does not apply — authority derives from the Selective Training and Service Act and War Labor Disputes Act, not an invoked emergency declaration.

Sec. 5's 60-day-after-restoration cap is a genuine, textually-explicit termination trigger — a materially stronger design feature than the fully open-ended structures in comparable seizure orders in this batch (e.g. EO 8944, EO 9570).

Sec. 2 directs federal agencies (National War Labor Board, War Manpower Commission, Selective Service System, Department of Justice) to cooperate with the Administrator; this is a coordination request, not removal of any agency's own oversight or judicial/congressional review.

No external party is given a role in checking whether the Administrator's determination of 'productive efficiency restored' is accurate or timely — the company has an obvious interest but no specified challenge mechanism.

By April 1945, wartime labor-dispute seizures under this statutory authority were an established, repeated pattern (see EO 8944, EO 9570 in this same corpus) — this order applies an existing template rather than establishing new precedent or authority.
Sec. 2's cooperation directive asks named agencies to support the Administrator; it does not redirect or override their own statutory missions.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Reconciliation pass (2026-07-04). Structural weight: 3/20 = 15.0%, clearly NOT SIGNIFICANT — lower than both new Gemini (16.7%) and far below new ChatGPT (54.55%, 3 CRITICAL). The 60-day cap in Sec. 5 is a real, specific limiting mechanism that materially distinguishes this order from its two sibling seizure orders in the batch; ChatGPT's coding does not appear to have weighted it.
